# orcasecurity_integration_coralogix (Resource)

Manages a [Coralogix](https://coralogix.com/) integration in Orca Security.

Orca stores Coralogix as a webhook variant: under the hood this resource creates
an external service config of `service_name = "webhook"` with `type = "coralogix"`.
You could achieve the same result with `orcasecurity_integration_webhook`, but
the dedicated resource hides the webhook plumbing and mirrors how the Orca UI
displays the integration.

## Example Usage

### Basic example

```terraform
# Coralogix integration
resource "orcasecurity_integration_coralogix" "example" {
  template_name = "coralogix_name"
  webhook_url   = "https://coralogix.us"
  api_key       = var.coralogix_api_key
  is_enabled    = true
  is_default    = false

  business_units = [
    "5308642b-f207-4610-a13b-f39c4db4a7a3",
    "d7a3b159-3063-433b-b954-0586ff3e8438",
  ]

  custom_headers = {
    key_header_1 = [{ custom = "value_header_1" }]
  }
}
```

### Sourcing the API key from HashiCorp Vault

```terraform
data "vault_generic_secret" "coralogix" {
  path = "secret/coralogix/orca"
}

resource "orcasecurity_integration_coralogix" "from_vault" {
  template_name = "coralogix-prod"
  webhook_url   = "https://coralogix.us"
  api_key       = data.vault_generic_secret.coralogix.data["api_key"]
}
```

## Argument Reference

* `template_name` — (Required, String) Identifier for the integration as shown
  in the Orca UI. Changing this forces a new resource.
* `webhook_url` — (Required, String) Coralogix ingest URL Orca posts events to.
* `api_key` — (Required, String, Sensitive) Coralogix API key sent with each
  request.
* `body_fields` — (Optional, List of String) Optional list of Orca alert fields
  to include in the request body.
* `custom_headers` — (Optional, Map of List of Object) Optional custom HTTP
  headers, keyed by header name. Each value is a list of
  `{ custom = "<value>" }` objects so a single header name can carry multiple
  values.
* `is_enabled` — (Optional, Bool) Defaults to `true`.
* `is_default` — (Optional, Bool) Defaults to `false`.
* `business_units` — (Optional, Set of String) Optional set of Orca business
  unit IDs that may use this integration.

## Attribute Reference

* `id` — (String) Orca external service config identifier (UUID).

## Notes

* The webhook `api_key` lives inside the JSON config (not in SSM), so Orca's
  GET response includes it. The provider still marks it as `sensitive = true`
  to keep it out of CLI output and never overwrites the planned value during
  Create or Update.
* Use an encrypted remote backend (S3 + KMS, GCS, Terraform Cloud, etc.) and
  restrict read access to the state — `api_key` and any sensitive header
  values land in state regardless of the `sensitive = true` flag.

## Import

```bash
terraform import orcasecurity_integration_coralogix.example coralogix_name
```
